mechanism_narrative: The first interest rate hike after a prolonged period of near-zero rates signals the end of maximum monetary accommodation. This increases the equity risk premium and discount rates on future earnings, causing a negative repricing of the S&P 500.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises for the first time after being <= 0.25% for at least 24 months
evidence_refs: fact:FOMC statements in late 2015 signal an imminent rate hike
series:FEDFUNDS has been near zero since 2008
regime_hint: ZIRP exit / start of tightening cycle
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following the first rate hike of a new cycle.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decrease in the unemployment rate reflects a tightening labor market and strong underlying economic demand. This translates into higher expected corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over a medium-term horizon.
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE has fallen from a peak of 10% in 2009 to 5.0%
failure_condition: The 6-month change in UNRATE has a statistically insignificant or positive correlation with 60-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales growth significantly outpaces industrial production growth, it indicates a potential imbalance where consumer demand is not being met by domestic production. This divergence is a negative signal for domestic corporate profitability and the broader market.
candidate_trigger: 6-month annualized % change in RSAFS > 6-month annualized % change in INDPRO by 3 percentage points
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO has been flat to down in recent months
failure_condition: Instances of RSAFS growth strongly outpacing INDPRO growth are not followed by negative S&P 500 returns on average over the subsequent 60 days (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A negative trend in industrial production indicates weakening momentum in the core productive sectors of the economy. This directly impacts the earnings of a significant portion of S&P 500 companies, leading to negative forward returns as expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO latest value 107.2123 is below levels from earlier in 2015
failure_condition: The 3-month momentum of INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive rank correlation with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below a critical threshold, it signals an overheating labor market. This stokes wage inflation fears and expectations of more aggressive monetary tightening, which negatively impacts equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE is at 5.0%, a post-GFC low
fact:Economists frequently debate the level of NAIRU, often estimated near 5%
failure_condition: Periods where UNRATE is below 5.1% are not followed by underperformance of the S&P 500 relative to periods where UNRATE is above 5.1%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts signals an acute contraction in the residential investment sector, a key leading indicator for the business cycle. This has negative spillover effects on broader economic activity and investor sentiment.
evidence_refs: series:HOUST latest value 1060.0 is a significant drop from the prior month's 1205.0
failure_condition: The S&P 500 does not show statistically significant negative returns in the 20 trading days following a >10% monthly drop in HOUST.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation, even from a low base, increases the perceived probability of future monetary policy tightening and erodes the real value of future corporate earnings, leading to a negative repricing of equities over very short horizons.
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL has been low but any uptick is watched closely ahead of a Fed hike
failure_condition: The 1-month change in CPIAUCSL does not have a persistent negative correlation with 5-day forward S&P 500 returns.

mechanism_narrative: When employment growth (PAYEMS) significantly outstrips growth in real output (INDPRO), it implies a decline in labor productivity. This trend pressures corporate profit margins as labor costs rise without a commensurate increase in production, leading to negative equity market performance.
candidate_trigger: 6-month % change in PAYEMS > 6-month % change in INDPRO by 2 percentage points
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS continues to show strong monthly gains
series:INDPRO has been stagnant or declining in late 2015
failure_condition: Periods of diverging PAYEMS and INDPRO growth are not followed by S&P 500 underperformance over the subsequent 60 trading days.
